<!--
Thanks for proposing a change to the bar. Anyone may — that's the point.
If this PR touches perfect_brief/rules/, scoring.yaml, or fixtures/, it is a
RULE CHANGE: fill every section below (empty sections stall the clock — see
GOVERNANCE.md § The clock). For engine/site/docs changes, fill "Why" and
delete the rest.
-->

## What kind of change

- [ ] **Rule change** — touches `perfect_brief/rules/`, `scoring.yaml`, or `fixtures/` (the 7-day discussion window applies)
- [ ] Engine / site / docs — non-normative (no window; normal review)

## Why — the rationale

<!-- Why does this predict a better brief or a smoother build? One honest
paragraph. "The current bar mis-scores X" with an example beats theory. -->

## What changes on the bar

| | before | after |
|---|---|---|
| Rule(s) touched | | |
| Weight | | |
| Severity | | |
| Gate membership | | |
| Threshold(s) in `scoring.yaml` | | |

## Evidence

<!-- References tiered per CONTRIBUTING.md: standard > practice > encyclopedic.
Pinned to version/clause. Authored and CHECKED BY YOU — not model-generated.
Encyclopedic sources may not be the sole anchor of a normative claim. -->

-

**Real brief that motivated this** (anonymised, or a fixture id):

## Corpus — the immune system

- [ ] `pytest` is green locally (the fixture corpus is part of the suite)
- [ ] No secrets, keys, or personal data anywhere in the diff
- Fixtures added or updated: <!-- a rule change without a fixture is an opinion -->
- Briefs whose band flips with this change, and why that's correct:

## Version impact

- [ ] patch — docs, references, typos (nothing normative moves)
- [ ] minor — weight/severity/criteria tweak, or a new rule
- [ ] major — a rule removed, gate membership changed, or a threshold moved

## The window

Rule-change PRs stay open **at least 7 calendar days** so the community can
read, test, and object. The clock restarts if the normative diff materially
changes. Merge needs: green corpus + a maintainer review + the window elapsed
+ no unresolved *actionable* objection. Declines come with reasons and the
door stays open for a return with new evidence. (GOVERNANCE.md § The clock.)
